content: I've been thinking about how to make my evenings more interesting. Running my own electrical business keeps me pretty busy during the day, but I need something creative to unwind.
That makes sense! What kind of creative outlets have you considered? I imagine working with electricity all day might influence what you're drawn to.
Funny you mention that - I've gotten really into 3D printing lately. It's amazing how I can design and print custom parts for my projects. Just printed some specialized wire holders last week.
That's brilliant! Combining your professional knowledge with 3D printing must open up so many possibilities. What printer do you use?
I've got an Ender 3 V2. Took me a while to get the calibration right, but now I'm printing at 0.12mm layer height. It's in my basement workshop alongside my aquarium setup.
An aquarium too? That's quite the setup! What kind of fish do you keep?
I've got a 75-gallon freshwater tank with some discus fish. They're beautiful but temperamental - reminds me of working with some of my commercial clients, actually. Everything has to be just right.
That's a great analogy! How long have you been in Chicago? I thought most electricians I know are still concentrated in traditional industrial areas.

I moved here from the Bay Area about two years ago. The cost of living was insane and I realized I could run my business from anywhere. Chicago's been good for business - lots of older buildings needing electrical work.
Smart move! What do you miss most about California, if anything?

Honestly, just the weather. Chicago winters are brutal. I've been trying to stay active with hiking when it's not too cold - there are some great forest preserves around here. Also picked up beekeeping last spring.
Beekeeping in Chicago? That's fascinating! How's that going?

Surprisingly well! I've got two hives in my backyard. Harvested about 60 pounds of honey last fall. The bees are fascinating to watch - it's almost meditative after a stressful day of dealing with permits and inspections.
60 pounds is impressive! Do you do anything with the honey or just enjoy it yourself?

I bottle some for friends and family, but most of it I use for home brewing. I've been experimenting with honey ale recipes - the latest batch turned out pretty good. Chess is another hobby I've picked up recently; there's a great club that meets downtown.
